topic: ACL main Contribution 4 gate-scope consistency polish

before_text_matches: page 2 line 094: 4. We bound material and stack checks in
page 2 line 095: one gate: NVIDIA material audit, 99
page 2 line 097: load/render checks. This gate supports
defect.reader_effect: The contribution collapsed material and stack checks into one gate, while the method and registry split them into separate evidence gates.

changes.summary: Changed Contribution 4 to keep material and stack checks scoped to named evidence, replacing in-one-gate wording with a check-level claim boundary.

rejected_iterations.summary: The first direct fix used claim-bounded wording and introduced visible page-2 contribution-column hyphenation: claim- / bounded and sup- / port.
The scoped-colon rewrite removed hyphenation but produced a visibly stretched contribution line before They support.

after_evidence.targeted_scan_result: Page 2 shows Contribution 4 as scoped to an NVIDIA material audit, 99 KuJiaLe VLN episodes, and repeated load/render checks, followed by bounded reliability claims.
after_evidence.full_scan_result: The targeted after scan finds no rejected claim- / bounded, sup- / port, en-, show-, offi-, ground-, behav-, sepa-, bench-, or speed- regressions in the edited contribution.
after_evidence.visual_result: The accepted after screenshot shows natural contribution-column line flow without the rejected hyphenation or stretched-line regressions.

claim_boundary: Contribution 4 prose only. No metrics, evidence pools, figures, tables, citations, metadata, or supported/forbidden claim scopes were changed.
residual_risk: Reference pages retain normal reference-column hyphenation; no new contribution-column linebreak defect was observed.
